meta.notes: SKELETON layer of the ELW Marriage rite, without communion. This layer is the robust, always-works foundation: it fixes the ordo structure, chrome, public-domain scripture reading slots, the public-domain traditional Lord's Prayer, and per-service variables. It bundles NO copyrighted ELW / Sundays & Seasons liturgical prose. Each canonical (copyrighted) section is an EMPTY keyed canonical_slot resolved at render time through core/content_source.resolve_text(section_key): a later pull layer auto-fills the canonical text from the church's licensed Sundays & Seasons account; failing that it falls back to a church-saved custom text; failing that it renders the entitlement placeholder. Source ordo verified against the Sundays & Seasons marriage rite (see docs/research/2026-07-liturgy-strategy/snapshots/marriage_no_communion.sha256.txt for the source snapshot hash and this rite's section_key list). Reading slots and the psalm are per-service variables filled from the chosen appointments; the vows name each partner from the per-service partner_one / partner_two variables.

blocks.note: Composite cover page from CongregationProfile (church name/address, cover image, service date). Identity/chrome, not liturgical text.
The minister addresses the assembly with the introduction to marriage.
The couple make their declaration of intention.
The minister prays the opening prayer.

blocks.title: First Reading
blocks.note: Chosen first reading; citation and text from the selected appointment.

blocks.title: Second Reading
blocks.note: Chosen second reading; citation and text from the selected appointment.

blocks.title: *GOSPEL
blocks.note: Chosen gospel; citation and text from the selected appointment.

blocks.text: The assembly is seated. The sermon follows.

blocks.title: *HYMN OF THE DAY
blocks.note: Hymn of the day; render mode is per-document renderer-side.
The couple exchange vows; the vows name each partner (per-service variables partner_one / partner_two).
The couple give and receive rings.
The minister announces the marriage and the assembly acclaims it.

blocks.title: *LORD'S PRAYER
blocks.note: Public-domain traditional 'Our Father' wording (elw.lords_prayer constant); always renders regardless of entitlement.
